meta.data_caveat: Realized data length is far shorter than the hypotheses' stated ~3.5yr. Actual spans: 30m series = 1001 bars (~3 weeks, 2026-06-29 to 2026-07-19); 1h BTC = 14001 bars (~1.6yr, 2024-12 to 2026-07); 1h ETH/SOL/HYPE/GOLD = 1001 bars (~6 weeks, 2026-06-08 to 2026-07-19). Short samples -> wide CIs, low statistical power.
meta.engine_caveat: The backtester's own WalkForward re-applies the IN-SAMPLE best_params to each OOS window (does NOT re-optimize per window). Therefore reported oos_sharpe is an inflated, non-true-OOS figure and should NOT be read as genuine out-of-sample robustness. True OOS requires per-window re-optimization. No lookahead was introduced: strategies use only t-1 and earlier; fills occur at the signal bar's close.
meta.mc_p_note: mc_p = approximate P(annual MC return <= 0), computed from 1000 bootstrap final_values (engine does not emit a formal p-value). No Newey-West HAC correction applied (autocorrelation/heteroskedasticity in returns unaddressed) -> significance estimates are optimistic.

cannot_verify.name: Cross-sectional relative-strength rotation (1d)
cannot_verify.reason: Requires a MULTI-ASSET engine: needs the 5 symbols aligned at the same daily bars to compute cross-sectional z-scores and rotate long winners / short losers. The current backtest engine (engine/backtester.py) processes a single symbol/timeframe DataFrame and goes long-only per symbol; it has no cross-sectional ranking, no portfolio of positions, and no shorting layer. Cannot be validated with the available single-asset engine. Needs: multi-symbol data join + cross-sectional scoring + portfolio/short execution.

cannot_verify.name: Inter-asset volatility-cointegration hedge (4h)
cannot_verify.reason: Requires a PAIRS/SPREAD engine: needs two (or more) cointegrated assets simultaneously to build the log-price spread, fit rolling OLS beta, z-score the spread, and trade long-A/short-B market-neutral. The current engine backtests one symbol at a time with no spread construction or simultaneous two-sided hedging. Cannot be validated with the available single-asset engine. Needs: paired-data feed + spread/OLS module + market-neutral execution.
